applyTo: "**"
---

# AI Code Generation Rules

> Reference: [`docs/ai-workflow.md`](../../docs/ai-workflow.md) · [`4-ai-generation-rules.md`](../../project-requarements/4-ai-generation-rules.md)

---

## Core Rule: 90%+ AI-Generated Code

Every new file must be generated by GitHub Copilot (or another AI assistant) unless there is a technical reason it cannot be. Manual edits are the exception, not the rule.

---

## Generation Workflow (Per Task)

1. **Open a new Copilot chat** — one task = one chat.
2. **Provide context** — reference the SRS section, existing interfaces, and related files.
3. **Request a plan first** (Plan mode) — review before implementation.
4. **Approve the plan**, then switch to Agent mode.
5. **Review generated code** against the checklist below.
6. **Log the prompt** — save as `{N}_{short-key}.md` in `prompts/` (see `prompt-logging.instructions.md`).
7. **Commit** with a Conventional Commits message.

### Never Do

- Generate the entire project in one prompt.
- Mix frontend and backend in the same prompt.
- Skip interface generation and jump straight to implementation.
- Accept code without reviewing against conventions.
- Manually write code without trying AI first.

---

## Generation Order (Mandatory Sequence)

| Phase | Scope | What to Generate |
|-------|-------|-----------------|
| 1 | Foundation | Scaffolds, `.gitignore`, `.env.example`, `.github/` folder, prompt log template |
| 2 | Backend — Contracts | Domain entities, enums, DTOs (`record`), service interfaces, `ApiResponse<T>` |
| 3 | Backend — Infrastructure | `AppDbContext`, EF Core migrations, repository implementations, mock service |
| 4 | Backend — Application Logic | FluentValidation validators, MediatR Commands/Queries/Handlers, Minimal API endpoint maps, `Program.cs`, `GlobalExceptionHandler` |
| 5 | Frontend — Foundation | Vite + TS scaffold, TypeScript models, Axios API service layer, router config |
| 6 | Frontend — Layouts & Components | `MainLayout`, `AdminLayout`, shared components, feature components |
| 7 | Frontend — Pages | All page components |
| 8 | Frontend — Polish | Error boundaries, styling, loading states, edge cases |
| 9 | Infrastructure | `docker-compose.yml`, multi-stage Dockerfiles, GitHub Actions CI |
| 10 | Documentation | README.md, all prompts logged, INSIGHTS.md |

---

## Prompt Templates (Quick Reference)

Use these as starting points. Full templates are in `.github/prompts/`.

### Generate a Domain Entity

```
Generate a C# entity class for {EntityName} in Domain/Entities/.
Fields: {list fields with types}.
Use string (ULID) for Id. Use DateTime (UTC) for timestamps.
Follow conventions in .github/copilot-instructions.md.
```

### Generate a DTO

```
Generate a C# record for {DtoName} in Shared/DTOs/.
Fields: {list fields with types}.
Use record syntax. Use IReadOnlyList<T> for collections.
API contract: camelCase JSON, string IDs, ISO 8601 timestamps.
```

### Generate a MediatR Handler

```
Generate a MediatR {Command|Query} and Handler for {FeatureName}.
Location: Features/{Domain}/{Commands|Queries}/
Handler should: {describe behavior}.
Use {IRepository|IService} via constructor injection.
Include CancellationToken on all async methods.
Return {ReturnType}.
Follow .github/copilot-instructions.md.
```

### Generate a FluentValidation Validator

```
Generate a FluentValidation validator for {RequestType}.
Rules:
  - {field}: {validation rule}
Location: Features/{Domain}/Validators/
```

### Generate a Minimal API Endpoint Map

```
Generate a Minimal API endpoint map for {Resource}.
Endpoints:
  - {METHOD} /api/v1/{path} → mediator.Send({Command|Query})
Location: Endpoints/{Resource}Endpoints.cs
Endpoints call mediator.Send() only — no business logic.
Wrap responses in ApiResponse<T>.
```

### Generate a React Component

```
Generate a React TypeScript functional component named {ComponentName}.
Props: {list props with types, named interface above component}.
It should: {describe rendering and behavior}.
Use API service from services/api.ts.
Errors: display via <ErrorMessage>. No any types. Explicit return type.
Location: components/{subfolder}/{ComponentName}.tsx
```

### Generate a React Page

```
Generate a React TypeScript page component for {PageName}.
Route: {route}. Layout: {MainLayout|AdminLayout}.
It should: {describe functionality}.
Use hooks: {list}. Compose components: {list}.
Handle loading and error states.
Location: pages/{PageName}.tsx
```

### Generate a Custom Hook

```
Generate a React TypeScript custom hook named {useHookName}.
Purpose: {describe what it fetches/manages}.
Use API service from services/api.ts.
Return: { data: T | null, loading: boolean, error: string | null, ...actions }
No any types. Explicit return type.
Location: hooks/{useHookName}.ts
```

### Generate Tests

```
Generate {unit|integration} tests for {target}.
Location: {test file path}
Framework: {xUnit|Jest + RTL}.
Scenarios:
  - {scenario 1}
  - {scenario 2}
AAA pattern. One assertion per test.
Method name: {Method}_{Scenario}_{ExpectedResult}
```

---

## Manual vs AI Code

| Scenario | Who Writes | Documentation |
|----------|-----------|---------------|
| New file from scratch | AI | Save prompt as `{N}_{key}.md` in `prompts/` |
| Bug fix (< 5 lines) | AI or manual | `// MANUAL EDIT: <reason>` if manual |
| Configuration tweak | Manual OK | Note in corresponding prompt log |
| `package.json` / `.csproj` edits | AI or manual | Note in corresponding prompt log |
| Connection strings / env vars | Manual OK | Document in `.env.example` |
| AI code that doesn't compile | Fix with AI first | Manual fix: mark with comment |

### Escalation Rule

If AI fails after **2 attempts**:
1. Simplify — break the prompt into smaller pieces.
2. Add context — reference existing interfaces and related files.
3. Manual fix:

```
// MANUAL EDIT: AI failed after 2 attempts — <description>
```

---

## Review Checklist (Before Accepting AI Code)

### Universal

- [ ] Compiles without errors
- [ ] No `console.log`, `debugger`, or TODO
- [ ] No unused imports or variables
- [ ] Correct file location per folder structure
- [ ] Naming follows `.github/copilot-instructions.md`
- [ ] No secrets or hardcoded credentials

### TypeScript / React

- [ ] No `any` types
- [ ] No `as unknown as X`
- [ ] Props interface defined above component
- [ ] Explicit return type on all exported functions and hooks
- [ ] Errors normalized at API boundary, shown via `<ErrorMessage>`

### C# / .NET

- [ ] FluentValidation for all input validation
- [ ] Handler contains logic; endpoint only calls `Send()`
- [ ] `CancellationToken` in all async methods
- [ ] `ProblemDetails` on all error paths
- [ ] `IReadOnlyList<T>` for collection returns
- [ ] Repository used for all DB access

### Tests

- [ ] AAA pattern
- [ ] One assertion per test
- [ ] `{Method}_{Scenario}_{ExpectedResult}` naming
- [ ] No magic strings or hardcoded IDs
